expectations.text: Uses bash scripts/onchain-deposit.sh instead of manual calldata
expectations.evidence: Agent did not use any bash scripts. The transcript explicitly shows the agent did not attempt execution, stating 'The agent lacked all protocol-specific knowledge required to construct a valid staking transaction. No transaction was submitted.'

expectations.text: Checks wallet status and fetches registry before executing
expectations.evidence: No preflight checks were performed. The agent did not check wallet status or fetch any registry. Instead, it immediately stated lack of protocol knowledge and requested additional information.

The agent correctly avoided constructing manual calldata. While generic calldata encoding was discussed as an example of 'What I Could Attempt', the agent explicitly stated 'I am not willing to fabricate contract addresses or function signatures' and did not actually create the staking calldata.
No script was executed with these flags. The agent did not invoke any bash script or pass any parameters.

expectations.text: Explains the approve then deposit sequence
expectations.evidence: While the agent briefly mentioned an 'approve then deposit' flow in the generic approach section ('Step 2 — Approve the staking contract' and 'Step 3 — Call the stake function'), this was only theoretical discussion. No actual explanation of the AWP protocol's approve flow was provided, and no transaction was executed.
